designs.approach: MINIMAL EXTRACTION — a plain (non-ECS) `EngineLoop` struct in vox_app that owns exactly the per-frame systems engine_runner ALREADY wires, sequenced by an explicit `step(&mut ctx)` with the same ordering as `handle_redraw`. Shells keep window/GPU/input/UI/win-condition; the loop owns sim+GI+physics+audio+scripts+animation. walking_sim adopts subsystems one at a time, never big-bang.
designs.where_it_lives: A NEW module `crates/vox_app/src/engine_loop.rs` (exposed via `vox_app::engine_loop`), NOT a new crate and NOT vox_core. Reason, proven from Cargo.toml: vox_core has ZERO deps on vox_render/vox_audio/vox_physics/vox_script/vox_ai (confirmed — its Cargo.toml lists none of them). But the systems we are unifying are exactly `vox_render::spectral_gi::SpectralRadianceCache` (engine_runner.rs:286,992-993), `vox_render::spectral_atmosphere::SpectralAtmosphere` (:285,990), `vox_physics::rapier::RapierPhysicsWorld` (:227,1969), `vox_audio::{AudioEngine,SpatialAudioManager}` (:223,245,1990-2006), `vox_render::shadows::ShadowMapper` (:254), `vox_script::{LuaRuntime,rhai_runtime}` (:263,307). Only vox_app depends on ALL of these (Cargo.toml:13-23). So vox_app is the lowest crate that CAN own this struct without adding new cross-crate dep edges (which would risk the 2087 green build). vox_core::engine_runtime::EngineRuntime stays exactly as-is and is HELD BY the loop (composition), not replaced.
designs.systems_owned:
MOVE INTO EngineLoop (sim-only, GPU-free, exactly what engine_runner already runs CPU-side):
1. Script/ECS fixed-step: `runtime.tick(dt)` (engine_runner:1922) — already a vox_core call, just relocated.
2. Procedural animation_system run (:1924-1931) — appends to RenderBuffer; loop drains it into FrameOutput.anim_splats.
3. Rapier physics: `physics.step()` + ECS<-Rapier transform sync (:1969-1987). NOTE: KCC character move (:1938-1962) stays SHELL-side (see below) because the two shells use DIFFERENT character types.
4. Audio: `audio.tick`/`set_listener` + `spatial_audio.tick`/`set_listener` (:1990-2006) and PlaySound command dispatch (:2008-2031).
5. Spectral GI: set_sky + `spectral_gi.propagate(splats,256)` + `apply` (:980-993) as `step_gi`, called by shell with its render-splat list.
6. Shadow update + sun_dir (:1063-1077) as `step_shadows`.
7. Rhai + Lua per-frame update & command drain (:2033-2080) — pure CPU, owned by loop behind `systems.scripts`.

STAY IN THE SHELLS (not in loop): window/Arc<Window>, WgpuBackend, GpuRasteriser, DLSS, SpectralFramebuffer/TemporalAccumulator, all `render_frame`/GPU submit (:878-2260), egui editor + gizmos + ScreenRay picking (:1856-1916), input collection (raw winit→InputState), camera controller, HUD/burn_text, tile streaming, particle EMITTERS owned by shell but ticked via shell (they feed splats to render), KCC character move (different per shell), win-condition/orbs (walking_sim only), patrol agents (engine_runner demo only — leave bin-local for now).
designs.how_shells_consume_it:
engine_runner (EDITOR shell): replace the EngineApp fields `engine, physics, audio, spatial_audio, shadow_mapper, spectral_atmosphere, spectral_gi` with a single `loop_: EngineLoop` (its `.runtime` re-exposes `.world`/`.time_of_day()` used at :974,981,1165,1178). `handle_redraw` calls `self.loop_.step_scripts(dt)`, `step_physics(dt)`, `step_audio(...)`, then KCC move stays inline (uses `vox_app::character_controller::CharacterController` + `self.loop_.physics`), then `render_frame` calls `self.loop_.step_gi(&render_splats, hour)` (replacing :990-993) and `step_shadows(...)` (replacing :1063-1077). Editor keeps egui/gizmos/picking/Rhai-Lua hot-reload UI bin-local. engine_runner DRIVES the loop by calling sub-steps in the existing order; nothing it does today is removed, only relocated.
walking_sim (GAME shell): adopts INCREMENTALLY behind SystemMask. It already owns `physics`+`audio`+`shadow_mapper`+`spectral_fb`. It uses `vox_core::character_controller::CharacterController` + `character_controller_tick` (walking_sim:21,541) and `physics.step()` (:550) and `shadow_mapper.update` (:578) and `audio.tick` (:591). Migration swaps each of those for the loop's identical sub-step one at a time. Win-condition (orb collection :594-630), bitmap HUD, windmill anim, and its WASD input stay 100% shell-specific. walking_sim keeps `gi:false, scripts:false` in its mask initially (it has no GI/Lua today), so adopting the loop adds ZERO new behavior — it just shares the physics/audio/shadow code paths.
designs.migration_steps:
Each step ends with `cargo build && cargo test` green (2087 tests) AND both bins still run.
S0. Add `crates/vox_app/src/engine_loop.rs` + `pub mod engine_loop;` in lib.rs. Define `EngineLoop`, `SystemMask`, `FrameCtx`, `FrameOutput`. Implement `new()`/sub-steps by COPYING the exact bodies from engine_runner (no behavior change). Add 3-4 unit tests on sub-steps (e.g. step_physics moves a dynamic body; step_gi changes a band). Nothing wired yet → build stays green.
S1. In engine_runner, construct `EngineLoop` ALONGSIDE existing fields but route ONLY `step_physics` through it (delete the inline :1969-1987 sync, call loop_.step_physics). Build/run/test green.
S2. Route engine_runner audio (:1990-2031) → `loop_.step_audio` + `drain_sound_commands`. Green.
S3. Route engine_runner GI (:980-993) → `loop_.step_gi`; shadows (:1063-1077) → `loop_.step_shadows`. Green.
S4. Route engine_runner scripts (:1922 + animation :1924-1931 + Rhai/Lua :2033-2080) → `loop_.step_scripts` (Rhai/Lua behind mask). Collapse the duplicated EngineApp fields into `loop_.*`. Green — engine_runner now FULLY on the loop.
S5. walking_sim: replace its `physics.step()` (:550) with `loop_.step_physics(dt)` (mask physics-only). Green; orbs still collectible.
S6. walking_sim: replace `audio.tick`/`set_listener` (:589-591) and `shadow_mapper.update` (:578) with loop sub-steps. Green.
S7. (Optional, opt-in) flip walking_sim `gi:true` to gain live GI for free — separate PR, easy revert.
Big-bang `step()`-only path is offered LAST as convenience; shells may keep calling sub-steps.
designs.green_keeping: 1. ZERO new crate-dep edges: the loop lives in vox_app which already depends on every needed crate (Cargo.toml:13-23), so no Cargo graph change → can't break the 2087-test build. 2. EngineRuntime/engine_runtime.rs is UNTOUCHED — all 30+ tests in that file (create_engine_and_spawn_entities…apply_force_changes_velocity, :931-1418) keep passing because the loop COMPOSES EngineRuntime, never edits it. 3. Sub-step bodies are copy-paste of existing engine_runner code, so byte-for-byte identical behavior; each migration step deletes the old inline copy only AFTER the call is wired, verified by running the bin. 4. SystemMask defaults make walking_sim adopt with NO new systems (gi/scripts off) so its 10-orb win flow is unchanged. 5. Track-S safety: the loop only CALLS public APIs of vox_render::spectral_gi/spectral_atmosphere/shadows — it never edits spectra-native/GPU files Track S owns. 6. New tests are added in S0 BEFORE any wiring, so the test count only grows.
designs.risks: 1. KCC character move differs per shell (engine_runner uses vox_app::character_controller + CharacterBody KCC :1938-1962; walking_sim uses vox_core::character_controller_tick :541) — so it MUST stay shell-side; if someone tries to pull it into the loop the two shells diverge. Mitigation: explicitly excluded above. 2. ECS<-Rapier sync (:1971-1987) queries `entity_rapier_bodies` which is a shell-owned map; step_physics needs that map passed in OR the map moves into the loop. Mitigation: move `entity_rapier_bodies`/`collider_to_entity` into EngineLoop (they're pure sim state). 3. RenderBuffer is in vox_core and the animation_system writes to it; loop must `std::mem::take` it (as :973-977 already does) and return via FrameOutput so the shell still gets anim splats — ordering-sensitive, covered by an S0 test. 4. Rhai global `drain_pending_commands()` (:2046) is process-global state; if both bins ran in one process it'd collide, but they're separate bins so fine. 5. Borrow friction: `step()` taking `&FrameCtx` while shell also holds `&mut self.camera` — kept clean by passing camera as plain Vec3 copies, not references into the shell.
designs.effort: ~2-3 days. Riskiest step is S4 (folding engine_runner's script+animation+Rhai+Lua block onto the loop and collapsing the duplicated fields) because that block reaches into `self.engine.world` and `RenderBuffer` in several places (:1922-2080) and the field collapse touches ~20 call sites across the 2691-line bin. S0 (the copy-out) is mechanical; S5-S6 (walking_sim) are small. Net diff is moderate and almost entirely additive + relocation, no algorithmic rewrites.

designs.approach: ECS-Schedule Unification with a host-hook boundary: turn vox_core's already-inline tick systems into a real bevy `Schedule` ordered with explicit `.before()/.after()`, and inject the engine-crate systems (Rapier physics, spectral GI, audio, navmesh AI, Lua/Rhai) as boxed `EngineSystem` trait objects stored in ECS resources that schedule wrapper-systems invoke at fixed ordering anchors. Both shells become thin ApplicationHandlers that only (a) push raw winit input into `InputResource`, (b) register their host systems + scripts, (c) call `engine.tick(dt)`, and (d) read `RenderBuffer`/`CameraState` to draw.
designs.where_it_lives: Lives in vox_core, extending the EXISTING `crates/vox_core/src/engine_runtime.rs` (EngineRuntime, World, FrameTime/FixedTime/RenderBuffer/CameraState/InputResource already there at lines 30-156, 599-815). Reason it must be vox_core and NOT a new vox_engine crate: the schedule has to own the bevy `World` plus all the components in `vox_core/src/ecs.rs` (TransformComponent, ScriptComponent, ColliderComponent, etc.) and the script systems (script_update_system @213, physics_integration_system @424, gather_splats_system @508) — these are already vox_core. Crucially, vox_core's Cargo deps are ONLY glam/uuid/half/bytemuck/serde/bevy_ecs/dashmap (confirmed crates/vox_core/Cargo.toml) — it CANNOT import vox_render/vox_physics/vox_audio/vox_script/vox_ai. So the heavy systems cannot move INTO vox_core as direct calls; they enter via a trait-object boundary (EngineSystem) defined in vox_core but IMPLEMENTED in vox_app. A new crate would force re-exporting all of ecs.rs and is unnecessary. Add `bevy_app = "0.16"` is NOT needed — plain `bevy_ecs::schedule::Schedule` suffices and vox_app already depends on bevy_app if richer plugin layering is ever wanted.
designs.systems_owned:
MOVE INTO the loop (run by the schedule, game-agnostic, already vox_core inline fns -> now scheduled): script_update_system, process_script_commands_system, physics_integration_system (velocity integrate), physics_collision_system (AABB), frustum_cull_system, gather_splats_system (engine_runtime.rs:213-550), plus the time-of-day advance.

ENTER the loop AS HOST HOOKS (impl EngineSystem in vox_app, registered per phase — keeps engine crates clean):
- PreSim: navmesh PatrolAgent update + SpectralPerception (engine_runner 1796-1809), biome ambient blend (1789-1793), camera intent.
- FixedSim: Rapier `physics.step()` + character KCC move_and_slide (1939-1969) — runs at fixed_dt with scripts.
- PostSim: Rapier-body->ECS transform sync (1971-1987), PlaySound command dispatch to spatial audio (2008-2031), rhai on_update (2033-2043), lua call_update + tick_thresholds (2066-2080), entity->splat delta sync (2082-2106).
- PreRender: spectral_gi.propagate/apply + set_sky (979-993), shadow_mapper.update (1063-1077), audio.tick/spatial_audio.tick (1990-2006), particle emitter tick (957-968), walk animation_system (1924-1931).

STAY SHELL-SPECIFIC (NOT in loop, called directly by each ApplicationHandler): GPU surface acquisition + present, DLSS upscale, egui render, gizmo/editor overlay burn_text, the SoftwareRasteriser/spectra_render call itself, window/title management. These are pure output and stay in render_frame()/handle_redraw after `tick()` returns, reading `engine.render_buffer()` + `engine.world`.
designs.how_shells_consume_it:
engine_runner (EDITOR shell): keeps its EngineApp struct but the per-frame systems move out of handle_redraw into registered hooks. In resumed()/setup it does `engine.add_system(PreSim, NavmeshAiSystem{...})`, `add_system(FixedSim, RapierStepSystem{physics, character_body})`, `add_system(PostSim, ScriptDispatchSystem{...})`, `add_system(PreRender, SpectralGiSystem{spectral_gi, atmosphere})` etc., wrapping the existing structs (RapierPhysicsWorld, SpectralRadianceCache, LuaRuntime) which the EngineSystem impls borrow via Arc<Mutex<>> or by living inside the hook struct. handle_redraw shrinks to: feed input -> `engine.set_camera(...)` -> `engine.tick(dt)` -> `render_frame()` (unchanged GPU/egui/editor path reading render_buffer). Editor-only concerns (gizmo drag, entity pick raycast @1855-1916, viewport modes, content browser) stay in the shell, gated by editor_visible — they are NOT registered as hooks.

walking_sim (GAME shell): today it bypasses EngineRuntime with its own update() (508-650). It adopts the SAME hooks but a smaller set — registers a CharacterControllerSystem (FixedSim) wrapping character_controller_tick + its Rapier ground raycast (541-568), a WindmillAnimSystem (PreRender), an OrbCollectSystem (PostSim) that owns the win condition. Its ApplicationHandler shrinks identically: feed WASD into input_mut(), set_camera from player yaw/pitch, tick(), then its bespoke render() (rasteriser + shadow + GameUI HUD) reading shared state. Win-condition, orb spawning, main-menu GameState, bitmap HUD stay 100% shell-specific (they are game rules, never engine).
designs.migration_steps:
Each step compiles + `cargo test` (2087) + both binaries run green before the next:

1. ADD-ONLY in vox_core: introduce EnginePhase, EngineSystem trait, HostSystems resource, and `add_system/input_mut/set_camera/render_buffer` methods. Insert HostSystems resource in `EngineRuntime::new`. Do NOT change tick() yet. Build is green; no behavior change; new unit test: register a counting EngineSystem, tick, assert it ran N times per phase with correct dt.

2. INTERNALIZE schedule WITHOUT moving host code: rewrite tick()'s body to build a bevy `Schedule` that calls the EXISTING inline systems (script_update_system, etc.) in the SAME order, plus the four empty wrapper systems that drain HostSystems (which is empty). Existing engine_runtime tests (engine_tick, fixed_timestep_*, apply_force_*, aabb_*) must pass unchanged — they assert on FixedStepCounter, CollisionPairs, velocities, all preserved. This proves the schedule reproduces the inline order exactly.

3. MIGRATE walking_sim FIRST (smaller, the ship target): create EngineRuntime inside WalkingSim, register CharacterControllerSystem/WindmillAnimSystem/OrbCollectSystem as hooks, route input through input_mut(). Move logic out of update() one hook at a time, each time deleting the now-dead inline code and re-running the binary. Keep render() untouched. walking_sim behavior (10 orbs, win) identical.

4. MIGRATE engine_runner hooks incrementally, ONE handle_redraw block per commit, in this safe order: PostSim audio/script dispatch -> PreSim navmesh AI -> PreRender GI/shadow -> FixedSim Rapier+KCC. After each extraction the block in handle_redraw is replaced by an `add_system` call done once in setup; the binary is run to confirm GI still propagates, agents still patrol, physics still steps.

5. CLEANUP: delete the now-empty orchestration scaffolding in both handle_redraw/update; main.rs (city-builder) is LEFT ALONE this milestone (it drives vox_sim; a later plan gives it a CityHook set). Document the phase contract in engine_runtime.rs.
designs.green_keeping: The 2087 tests touch vox_core's inline systems via EngineRuntime public API (engine_runtime.rs tests @916-1419 assert on FixedStepCounter, CollisionPairs, VelocityComponent, RenderBuffer, ScriptInstances). Green-keeping tactics: (1) Steps 1-2 are pure-additive/refactor — the schedule in step 2 calls the IDENTICAL system fns in the IDENTICAL order, and the fixed-loop accumulator math (lines 690-704) is preserved verbatim, so FixedStepCounter and accumulator-remainder tests (@1111,1162) stay exact. (2) Keep script_update -> process_script_commands -> physics_integration -> physics_collision adjacency inside FixedSim so apply_force_changes_velocity_by_real_delta (@1352, depends on dt=1/60 ordering) and destroy_command_despawns (@1314) keep their semantics. (3) HostSystems starts empty, so existing tests that never register hooks see zero behavior change. (4) Binaries stay green because migration moves code without changing call order: each extracted hook is the SAME code at the SAME phase anchor, verified by running the binary after each block. (5) Don't touch any vox_render spectra-native/GPU file — the GI hook only *calls* SpectralRadianceCache::propagate/apply (Track S owns the impl), wrapping not editing it.
designs.risks: 1. Borrow/aliasing: host hooks need exclusive World AND their own non-ECS state (RapierPhysicsWorld, SpectralRadianceCache). Mitigation: hook owns that state as struct fields (Box<dyn EngineSystem>), so `run(&mut world, dt)` has clean access; no double-borrow of World. 2. The render-phase GI (propagate/apply @992-993) currently runs on render_splats built AFTER cull/LOD INSIDE render_frame, not on the ECS gather buffer — so GI as a PreRender hook must operate on the same splat list; mitigation: keep GI in the shell's render_frame initially (it reads render_buffer), only promote to a hook once gather produces the full list. 3. Fixed-vs-frame dt: Rapier step today runs once per frame (1969), not per fixed step; moving it to FixedSim changes it to per-substep. Mitigation: register Rapier at a new `FrameSim`-style single-run path OR keep it frame-rate by registering at PostSim (variable dt) to preserve current behavior, deferring true fixed physics to a later plan. 4. Lua/Rhai global pending-command queues (drain_pending_commands) are process-global; ordering across hooks must match today's. 5. main.rs city-builder diverges (bevy_app App, not EngineRuntime) — explicitly out of scope this milestone to avoid a big-bang.
designs.effort: Medium-to-large but cleanly incremental: ~5 commits. Steps 1-2 (vox_core schedule + trait boundary) are ~1 day and the riskiest single piece because every one of the 2087 tests routes through tick() — if the schedule reorders systems even slightly, fixed-step/force/collision tests break; this is de-risked by reusing the exact inline fns in the exact order. Step 3 (walking_sim) ~1 day. Step 4 (engine_runner hook extraction) is the bulk, ~2-3 days, but each of the 4 phase extractions is independently verifiable by running the binary, so risk is spread. Riskiest step overall: Step 2 (schedule internalization) — it is the one change where a subtle ordering or dt mismatch silently breaks many tests at once.

designs.approach: Trait-based System Registry ("EngineLoop"): a game-agnostic `EngineLoop` in vox_core owns an ordered `Vec<Box<dyn EngineSystem>>` plus a typed blackboard (`EngineCtx`); each per-frame subsystem (GI, physics, audio, AI/navmesh, scripts, animation, render-submit) is one `EngineSystem` living in the engine layer; walking_sim and engine_runner register the systems they want plus their own shell-specific ones (win-condition vs gizmos) and keep owning winit/input/UI. Thesis: extract the *ordering and dispatch*, not the subsystems — the loop is a sequencer over boxed systems, so a shell is just "which systems, in what order."
designs.where_it_lives: The TRAIT + sequencer (`EngineSystem`, `EngineLoop`, `EngineCtx`, `SystemId`) live in a NEW module `vox_core/src/engine_loop.rs` (re-exported `pub mod engine_loop`). Reason: vox_core's deps are only glam/uuid/half/bytemuck/serde/bevy_ecs/dashmap (confirmed in crates/vox_core/Cargo.toml) — no winit/render/audio — so the trait stays game-agnostic per CLAUDE.md's "engine crates must never contain game concepts." But the CONCRETE systems (GiSystem, PhysicsSystem, AudioSystem, ScriptSystem, AnimationSystem) live in the existing `ochroma_engine` crate (crates/ochroma_engine/src/, currently a pure re-export facade with deps on vox_render/vox_audio/vox_physics/vox_script/vox_terrain/vox_sim/vox_net/vox_nn but NOT winit/egui/vox_ai). Those crates are exactly what the systems touch, so a new `ochroma_engine::systems` module is the natural home and keeps vox_core clean. vox_app must add `ochroma_engine = { path = "../ochroma_engine" }` to its Cargo.toml (it currently does not depend on it). AI/navmesh patrol uses vox_ai, which neither vox_core nor ochroma_engine depends on — so the AiSystem stays in vox_app (it's also arguably game/demo logic, not engine), registered shell-side. NO file under vox_render's spectra-native/GPU path is touched; GiSystem only calls the CPU `SpectralRadianceCache::{new,set_sky,propagate,apply}` in crates/vox_render/src/spectral_gi.rs lines 69-178 (the GPU variant at line 244 is untouched, Track S owns it).
designs.systems_owned:
MOVE INTO loop systems (shared, identical work today in both shells or only in engine_runner): (1) PhysicsSystem — `RapierPhysicsWorld::step()` (engine_runner line 1969, walking_sim line 550) + ECS transform sync (engine_runner 1971-1987). (2) AudioSystem — `AudioEngine::tick`+`set_listener` and `SpatialAudioManager::tick`+`set_listener` (engine_runner 1990-2006, walking_sim 589-591). (3) ScriptSystem — `engine.tick(dt)` ECS script step, Rhai `on_update`+drain (engine_runner 2033-2064), Lua `call_update`+`tick_thresholds` (2066-2080). (4) AnimationSystem — the `animation_system` bevy run (engine_runner 1924-1931); walking_sim's Windmill::tick is the same shape. (5) GiSystem — `spectral_gi.set_sky/propagate(render_splats,256)/apply` (engine_runner 979-993); this is the headline capability walking_sim lacks and gains for free. (6) Optional TimeOfDaySystem — sun_zenith/illuminant from `engine.time_of_day()` (engine_runner 979-991, 1061-1067).
STAY SHELL-SIDE (per shell, not in the loop): camera update from input (engine_runner update_camera 836-872; walking_sim 518-547 char move) — input is shell-owned; the actual GPU render + DLSS + HUD/tonemap + present (engine_runner render_frame 878+, walking_sim render 652+) — render is the shell's surface/backend; AI/navmesh PatrolAgent (engine_runner 1795-1809, uses vox_ai which the engine crate doesn't depend on) registered only by engine_runner as a shell system; egui editor + gizmos + pick/place (engine_runner 1842-1916) editor-only; win-condition + orb collection + GameUI (walking_sim 594-650) game-only; LOD tile streaming + notifications (engine_runner 1814-1840) editor-only for now. Frustum cull/LOD/shadow-mask currently live inside render_frame and stay with render (shell) until render itself is unified later — out of scope here.
designs.how_shells_consume_it:
Both shells keep their winit `ApplicationHandler`, their own `Window`/`WgpuBackend`/input sets, and their own `render()`/`render_frame()`. In `RedrawRequested`/`handle_redraw` each shell: (a) computes dt and reads input into the existing `EngineRuntime.world` resources + writes shell state (camera, time-of-day, the assembled `render_splats` Vec) into `engine_loop.blackboard_mut()` typed slots; (b) calls `engine_loop.run_frame(dt, &mut self.engine.world)` which runs the registered systems in order (GiSystem mutates the RenderSplats slot in place, PhysicsSystem syncs ECS, etc.); (c) reads results back from the blackboard (post-GI splats, listener pose) and calls its own `render()` to draw + present.
walking_sim (GAME shell): in `new()` builds `EngineLoop` and registers PhysicsSystem(its existing `RapierPhysicsWorld`), AudioSystem(its `SpatialAudioManager`), AnimationSystem, ScriptSystem(its `RhaiRuntime`), and — NEW capability — GiSystem (giving it live spectral GI it never had). Keeps win-condition, orb collection, GameUI, CharacterController move, Windmill (registered as a tiny shell `WindmillSystem` or left inline) as shell code. `update()` shrinks to: read input -> set blackboard -> `engine_loop.run_frame` -> orb/win check -> HUD text.
engine_runner (EDITOR shell): registers the SAME engine systems from the SAME `ochroma_engine::systems`, PLUS shell systems it owns: AiSystem (vox_ai patrol), and keeps editor pick/place/gizmo/egui/LOD-streaming inline in `handle_redraw`. It tunes systems via `engine_loop.system_mut::<GiSystem>(SystemId("gi"))` to set `max_emitters`/atmosphere from time-of-day each frame. Shell-specific = input mapping, editor UI/gizmos, camera fly-cam, AI demo, networking replication, DLSS path.
designs.migration_steps:
Strangler-fig, one system at a time; `cargo build && cargo test` green after EACH step.
STEP 0: Add `engine_loop.rs` to vox_core with `EngineSystem`, `EngineLoop`, `Blackboard`, `SystemId` + unit tests (a fake counter system proves ordering/run_frame). Nothing wired into shells yet. Green: new module compiles, ~6 new tests pass, 2087 untouched.
STEP 1: Create `ochroma_engine::systems` module + add `ochroma_engine` dep to vox_app/Cargo.toml. Implement ONE system first — `PhysicsSystem` wrapping `RapierPhysicsWorld` — with a test that stepping the system advances a body identically to calling `world.step()` directly. Not yet called from shells. Green.
STEP 2: In walking_sim ONLY, construct an `EngineLoop`, register `PhysicsSystem`, and REPLACE the inline `self.physics.step()` (line 550) with `self.engine_loop.run_frame(...)` driving that one system; move `RapierPhysicsWorld` ownership into the system (or have the system borrow via blackboard). Run walking_sim manually + tests. Green. engine_runner untouched.
STEP 3: Repeat STEP 2's swap in engine_runner for `physics.step()` (line 1969) — but keep the ECS-sync and character-KCC code inline for now (it reads `self.physics` fields; expose them via the system or keep that block calling into the system's `world` accessor). Green.
STEP 4: Add `AudioSystem`; swap walking_sim 589-591 and engine_runner 1990-2006. STEP 5: `ScriptSystem`; swap the Rhai/Lua/engine.tick blocks. STEP 6: `AnimationSystem`; swap the bevy animation run. STEP 7: `GiSystem`; swap engine_runner 979-993, and ADD it to walking_sim (its new capability) — gate behind a flag if perf-sensitive. STEP 8: engine_runner registers shell-owned `AiSystem` (vox_ai) replacing the inline patrol loop 1795-1809.
After each step the replaced inline code is deleted only once its system passes its test and the binary still renders. Final state: both `handle_redraw`/`update` reduce to input->blackboard->`run_frame`->render, with shell-only code (UI, win-cond, gizmos) remaining.
designs.green_keeping: The 2087 tests live in the library crates (vox_core, vox_render, etc.) and in `engine_runtime.rs` tests; they assert on `EngineRuntime::tick` + ECS systems, which this design does NOT modify — `EngineRuntime` stays exactly as-is and ScriptSystem simply *calls* `engine.tick(dt)` rather than replacing it, so all 30+ engine_runtime tests (e.g. `script_state_persists_across_ticks`, `apply_force_changes_velocity_by_real_delta`, `gather_splats_fills_render_buffer`) keep passing untouched. New code is additive: a new vox_core module and a new ochroma_engine module, each with its OWN tests that check real computed outcomes (e.g. PhysicsSystem test asserts a body's y after N frames equals the direct-`step()` y to 1e-5; the loop test asserts systems ran in registration order by appending SystemIds to a Vec). Per-step, only ONE inline call site in ONE binary is swapped, and the swapped-out behaviour is preserved by delegating to the same underlying object (same `RapierPhysicsWorld`, same `SpectralRadianceCache`) — so a binary either compiles+behaves identically or the step is reverted. Binaries are validated by `cargo build --bin walking_sim` / `--bin ochroma` after every step (compile-green) plus a manual smoke run; no test currently drives the winit loop so the binaries' green-ness is build+manual, which is the existing bar. Crucially: do NOT touch vox_render spectra-native/GPU files — GiSystem references only `vox_render::spectral_gi::SpectralRadianceCache` CPU methods, which are already public and called by engine_runner today, so no API change in Track S's files.
designs.risks: (1) Ownership/borrow: today engine_runner's loop freely interleaves `self.physics`, `self.engine.world`, `self.camera` in one method; once systems own those objects, the cross-system reads (e.g. character-KCC reads physics AND camera AND character) need data routed through the Blackboard, which can get awkward for the tightly-coupled KCC block — mitigate by keeping KCC shell-side initially (STEP 3 note) and only moving the bare `step()`. (2) RenderSplats data flow: GI mutates the splat list that the SHELL then renders; passing a `Vec<GaussianSplat>` through the blackboard each frame risks a clone/alloc — mitigate with `get_or_insert_with` + `std::mem::take`/`std::mem::swap` (the codebase already uses `std::mem::take` on RenderBuffer, engine_runner 973). (3) `'static`/`Send` bounds on boxed systems vs subsystem objects that may hold non-Send handles (audio backend, wgpu) — AudioEngine/SpatialAudioManager and GI cache are CPU/Send, but verify before boxing; drop the `Send` bound on Blackboard if a system must hold a non-Send handle (the loop is single-threaded anyway). (4) Order sensitivity: GI must run AFTER particles/animation append splats but BEFORE render (engine_runner order 956->992->render); `insert_before/insert_after(SystemId)` exists precisely to encode this, but a shell registering in the wrong order silently breaks GI — mitigate with a debug-assert that GiSystem precedes the render-read. (5) engine_runner's render_frame currently does GI *inside* render_frame, not in a pre-render block — extracting GiSystem means the splat assembly (lines 908-977) must move before run_frame or the blackboard must carry the assembled list; this is the fiddliest single step (STEP 7) and should be done last.
designs.effort: Medium overall (~3-5 days). STEP 0-1 (trait + first system + tests) ~0.5 day and low-risk. Per-system swap steps (2-6) ~0.5 day each, mechanical. RISKIEST: STEP 7 (GiSystem) because engine_runner does GI inside `render_frame` over a locally-assembled `render_splats` Vec (lines 908-993), so extracting it forces moving splat assembly out of render and threading the Vec through the blackboard — this is the one step that can break the editor's visuals; do it last, behind a feature flag, with a before/after screenshot check. STEP 3 (physics in engine_runner) is second-riskiest due to KCC coupling. The vox_core trait module and ochroma_engine systems module are both purely additive, so the 2087 tests are never at risk from the infrastructure itself — risk is concentrated in the per-binary call-site swaps.

recommendation.recommended: Approach #1 (MINIMAL EXTRACTION — a plain non-ECS `EngineLoop` struct in `crates/vox_app/src/engine_loop.rs`), grafted with two ideas from the losers: (a) a tiny typed `Blackboard` from #3 to carry the post-GI render-splat list and listener pose between sub-steps without a god-struct, and (b) the named `EnginePhase` ordering vocabulary from #2 used purely as documentation/anchors for the explicit `step()` sequence. The loop OWNS the CPU subsystems engine_runner already wires (GI, physics+ECS sync, audio, scripts/animation, shadows) and COMPOSES the existing `vox_core::engine_runtime::EngineRuntime` rather than replacing it. Shells keep window/GPU/input/UI/win-condition/KCC.
recommendation.rationale: Three confirmed facts force this choice. (1) vox_core's Cargo.toml depends ONLY on glam/uuid/half/bytemuck/serde/bevy_ecs/dashmap/serde_json/toml — it CANNOT import vox_render/vox_audio/vox_physics/vox_script. So the concrete subsystems (SpectralRadianceCache, RapierPhysicsWorld, AudioEngine, RhaiRuntime) can ONLY live where every dep already exists: vox_app (its Cargo.toml lists all of them). #2 puts the orchestration in vox_core but is then forced to inject every heavy system through a `Box<dyn EngineSystem>` trait boundary — pure indirection that buys nothing today, and its riskiest step ('schedule internalization', rewriting tick() so all 2087 tests route through a new bevy Schedule) puts the whole green build at risk in ONE commit. #3 routes concrete systems through a NEW `ochroma_engine` crate and adds `ochroma_engine` to vox_app/Cargo.toml — a new cross-crate dep edge, exactly the kind of Cargo-graph change that can break the green build, for a facade crate vox_app doesn't currently use. (2) engine_runner ALREADY calls `self.engine.tick(dt)` (vox_core EngineRuntime) at line 1922 and runs its extra subsystems AROUND it as inline blocks (physics 1969-1987, audio 1990-2031, GI 980-993, shadows 1063-1077, scripts/rhai/lua 2033-2080). So the shared work is literally already sequenced — extraction is relocation, not rewrite, which is the cheapest path to staying green. (3) walking_sim holds NO EngineRuntime and uses a DIFFERENT character path (`vox_core::character_controller_tick`) than engine_runner (`vox_app::character_controller` + Rapier KCC). A loop that tried to own KCC (as #2/#3 propose putting it in a FixedSim hook) would force the two shells to converge on one character type — a behavior change. #1 explicitly leaves KCC shell-side, so adoption is purely additive. #1 also keeps EngineRuntime UNTOUCHED, so all 30+ engine_runtime.rs tests (FixedStepCounter, CollisionPairs, apply_force_changes_velocity_by_real_delta @1352, gather_splats_fills_render_buffer) cannot regress. Net: #1 introduces ZERO new crate-dep edges, touches ZERO vox_core/Track-S files in its core steps, and every migration step is a relocate-then-delete, which is the only one of the three that lets the first three commits each be trivially green.
recommendation.where_it_lives: NEW module `crates/vox_app/src/engine_loop.rs`, exposed via `pub mod engine_loop;` in `crates/vox_app/src/lib.rs` (which already declares ~30 pub modules, e.g. character_controller, walk_animation, systems). NOT a new crate, NOT vox_core, NOT ochroma_engine. Proof it must be vox_app: the loop directly names `vox_render::spectral_gi::SpectralRadianceCache` (engine_runner.rs:286,992-993), `vox_render::spectral_atmosphere::SpectralAtmosphere` (:285,990), `vox_physics::rapier::RapierPhysicsWorld` (:230,1969), `vox_audio::AudioEngine` + `vox_audio::spatial::SpatialAudioManager` (:245,1990-2006), `vox_render::shadows::ShadowMapper` (:254,1067). vox_core/Cargo.toml depends on NONE of those, so it cannot host this struct; vox_app/Cargo.toml already depends on ALL of them, so no Cargo-graph change is needed (the single most important green-keeping property). `EngineRuntime` in crates/vox_core/src/engine_runtime.rs stays byte-for-byte unchanged and is HELD as `EngineLoop.runtime` (composition). The CPU GI path used (`SpectralRadianceCache::{new@69,set_sky@78,propagate@137,apply@178}`) is fully separate from the wgpu `GpuGiPass@234` that Track S owns — the loop never references any `wgpu::Device`-bearing type, so it cannot collide with Track S's spectra-native/GPU files.
recommendation.migration_plan:
Every step ends with `cargo build --workspace && cargo test --workspace` GREEN (2087 tests, count only grows) AND both `--bin ochroma` and `--bin walking_sim` still build+run. Each step is relocate-then-delete: wire the new call, confirm green, THEN delete the old inline copy.

S0 (additive scaffold — see first_3_steps for exact commit).
S1 (engine_runner physics): construct `EngineLoop` ALONGSIDE the existing EngineApp fields (do NOT collapse fields yet). Move `entity_rapier_bodies` into the loop. Replace the inline physics block (engine_runner 1969-1987) with `self.loop_.step_physics(dt)`. KCC move (1938-1962) stays inline above it (uses vox_app::character_controller). Green; agents/physics unchanged.
S2 (engine_runner audio): replace 1990-2006 with `self.loop_.step_audio(dt, listener_pos, listener_fwd)` and 2008-2031 with `self.loop_.drain_sound_commands()`. Green.
S3 (engine_runner GI+shadows): in render_frame, replace 980-993 with `let render_splats = self.loop_.step_gi(&render_splats, hour);` and 1063-1077 with `let sun_dir = self.loop_.step_shadows(cam_pos, cam_fwd, hour, &shadow_splats);`. Green; GI still propagates (verify visually + the S0 band-change test guards the math).
S4 (engine_runner scripts+animation): replace `self.engine.tick(dt)` (1922) + animation_system block (1924-1931) with `self.loop_.step_scripts(dt)` and drain `FrameOutput.anim_splats`. Then COLLAPSE the now-duplicated EngineApp fields (engine, physics, audio, spatial_audio, shadow_mapper, spectral_atmosphere, spectral_gi, entity_rapier_bodies) into `self.loop_.*`, updating the ~20 call sites (974,981,1165,1178, etc.) to go through `loop_.world()/loop_.time_of_day()`. Rhai/Lua hot-reload (2033-2080) stays editor-shell-local behind mask.scripts. Green — engine_runner now FULLY on the loop. (This is the riskiest commit; it is LAST for engine_runner so every prior step de-risks it.)
S5 (walking_sim physics): walking_sim has NO EngineRuntime today, so first give it one: construct `EngineLoop::new(cfg, SystemMask::game_minimal())` in WalkingSim::new (gi=false, scripts=false). Replace `self.physics.step()` (walking_sim:550) + (no ECS sync needed, it has none yet) with `self.loop_.step_physics(dt)`, moving its RapierPhysicsWorld into the loop. character_controller_tick (541) and orb logic (594-630) stay shell-side. Green; 10 orbs still collectible.
S6 (walking_sim audio+shadows): replace `self.audio.tick`/`set_listener` (589-591) with `self.loop_.step_audio(...)` and `self.shadow_mapper.update` (578) with `self.loop_.step_shadows(...)`. Green. Both shells now share physics/audio/shadow code paths.
S7 (OPTIONAL, separate easily-revertable PR): flip walking_sim mask `gi:true` and call `loop_.step_gi` in its render() — walking_sim gains live spectral GI for free. Standalone so it never blocks the unification.
main.rs (city-builder, vox_sim) is OUT OF SCOPE this milestone (it drives bevy_app/vox_sim, not EngineRuntime); a later plan gives it a CityHook set. Big-bang `step()`-only path is a convenience offered AFTER S6 — shells may keep calling sub-steps forever.
first_3_steps: COMMIT 1 (S0a, scaffold only): Add crates/vox_app/src/engine_loop.rs defining EnginePhase, SystemMask, Blackboard, FrameCtx, FrameOutput, and the EngineLoop struct with `new`, `with_mask`, `world`, `time_of_day`, `set_time_of_day` and EMPTY-but-correct sub-step bodies that COPY the exact code from engine_runner (step_physics from 1969-1987, step_audio from 1990-2006, step_gi from 980-993, step_shadows from 1063-1077, step_scripts from 1922+1924-1931). Add `pub mod engine_loop;` to lib.rs. Nothing in any binary calls it yet. Green: new module compiles, ZERO call-site changes, 2087 tests untouched, both bins build.

COMMIT 2 (S0b, prove the sub-steps with real assertions): Add 4 unit tests in engine_loop.rs that check REAL computed outcomes (never is_some()): (1) step_physics: spawn a dynamic Rapier body at y=10, run step_physics 60x at dt=1/60, assert final y is strictly < 10.0 by gravity (compare to a direct RapierPhysicsWorld::step() reference to ~1e-4). (2) step_gi: build 2 splats (one opaque emitter), call step_gi, assert a specific output band differs from input by the propagate/apply formula. (3) step_scripts: register a counting GameScript via runtime, step_scripts 3x, assert counter==3 and FixedStepCounter matches. (4) blackboard round-trip: insert Vec<GaussianSplat>, get_mut, mutate, get, assert mutation persisted. Green: test count = 2087+4 = 2091, both bins build.

COMMIT 3 (S1, first real call-site swap, engine_runner physics): In engine_runner construct `loop_: EngineLoop` alongside existing fields, move `entity_rapier_bodies` into it, and replace ONLY the inline physics block (1969-1987) with `self.loop_.step_physics(dt)`. Delete the old inline block in the SAME commit after wiring. KCC move (1938-1962), audio, GI, scripts all still inline/unchanged. Green: `cargo build --bin ochroma` compiles, 2091 tests pass, manual smoke run shows physics still steps and patrol agents still move.
recommendation.risks: 1. FIELD COLLAPSE FANOUT (S4): folding engine_runner's 8 EngineApp fields into `loop_.*` touches ~20 call sites (974,981,1165,1178,1332,1375-1378,1544, etc.) that read self.engine.world / self.engine.time_of_day. Mitigation: `loop_.world()` and `loop_.time_of_day()` re-expose them with identical semantics; do this collapse LAST (S4) so every prior step has already proven the loop works; it is mechanical rename, not logic change. 2. KCC DIVERGENCE: engine_runner uses vox_app::character_controller + Rapier move_and_slide (1938-1962); walking_sim uses vox_core::character_controller_tick (541). These MUST stay shell-side — explicitly excluded from EngineLoop. If a future change tries to pull KCC into the loop the shells diverge; guard with a comment in engine_loop.rs. 3. ECS<-RAPIER SYNC needs entity_rapier_bodies, a shell map. Mitigation: relocated INTO EngineLoop in S1 (it is pure sim state, not shell state). 4. RHAI/LUA GLOBAL STATE: rhai_runtime::drain_pending_commands() (2046) is process-global; not put in EngineLoop's core API — stays editor-shell-local behind mask.scripts so the two bins (separate processes) never collide. 5. GI ORDERING: step_gi must run on the shell's POST-cull/LOD render-splat list (assembled in render_frame 908-977), AFTER particles+animation append, BEFORE the rasteriser reads. Mitigation: step_gi is a sub-step the shell calls explicitly at the right point in render_frame (not in step()), and the Blackboard carries the list if needed; the S0b band-change test guards the math. 6. BORROW FRICTION: step() takes &FrameCtx while shell holds &mut self.camera. Mitigation: camera passed by Vec3 value, never by reference into the shell. 7. AUDIO/PHYSICS Send-ness for the boxed Blackboard: AVOIDED — Blackboard stores `Box<dyn Any>` with NO Send bound (loop is single-threaded), and the audio/physics handles live as named struct fields, not in the blackboard, so non-Send internals never matter. 8. TRACK S: the loop only CALLS public CPU methods of spectral_gi/spectral_atmosphere/shadows and never references any wgpu::Device type, so it cannot touch spectra-native/GPU files.
recommendation.grafted_ideas: From #3 (Trait-based registry): (a) the typed `Blackboard` (TypeId->Box<dyn Any> map with insert/get/get_mut) is adopted verbatim as `EngineLoop.blackboard` to pass the post-GI render-splat list and listener pose between sub-steps WITHOUT a god-struct or per-frame clones — using get_mut + std::mem::take exactly as the codebase already does on RenderBuffer (engine_runner 973). (b) #3's `SystemId`/enabled() idea is distilled into the simpler `SystemMask` opt-out flags so walking_sim adopts shared code paths with gi/scripts OFF (zero new behavior), and a shell can flip GI on with one bool (S7). From #2 (ECS-Schedule): (a) the `EnginePhase` named-ordering vocabulary (Scripts->Physics->Audio->Gi->Shadows) is borrowed as DOCUMENTATION/anchor names that describe WHEN each sub-step runs, making step() self-documenting and giving tests stable phase names — without paying #2's cost of actually rewriting tick() into a bevy Schedule (which risked all 2087 tests in one commit). (b) #2's discipline of 'keep script_update -> process_script_commands -> physics_integration -> physics_collision adjacency so apply_force_changes_velocity_by_real_delta stays exact' is honored by NOT touching EngineRuntime::tick at all — step_scripts just calls runtime.tick(dt), so that adjacency and the fixed-step accumulator math (690-704) are preserved by construction. From #2's risk analysis: the insight that Rapier currently steps once-per-FRAME (1969), not per fixed-substep, is respected — step_physics is a per-frame call (variable dt), preserving today's behavior; true fixed-step physics is explicitly deferred to a later plan.
